description: DEBUG ONLY — 20 Q&A pairs grounded in CodeGraphRAG source code. NEVER USE FOR REPORTED EXPERIMENTS. Use eval/benchmark_dataset.json for all published results.

repositories.name: CodeGraphRAG
repositories.description: The CodeGraphRAG repository itself — evaluated on its own source code.

items.question: What chunking strategy is configured in config.yaml?
items.answer: The chunking strategy is ast_semantic_overlap, configured under the chunking section of config.yaml.

items.question: What is the default embedding model used in the vector store?
items.answer: The default embedding model is intfloat/e5-base-v2, loaded from config.yaml by VectorStoreManager.
items.notes: Verifiable from storage/vector_store.py line 17 and config.yaml line 7

items.question: What vector database is used to store chunk embeddings?
items.answer: Qdrant is used as the vector database, with an embedded local disk-based deployment configured by qdrant_path in config.yaml.

items.question: Which function in the orchestrator writes a log entry after every query?
items.answer: The _write_log function in agents/orchestrator.py appends a JSON entry to logs/query_logs.jsonl after every query.

items.question: What algorithm is used for diversity-aware reranking in the retrieval pipeline?
items.answer: Maximal Marginal Relevance (MMR) is used for diversity-aware reranking, implemented in retrieval/mmr_rerank.py.

items.question: What graph library is used to store the knowledge graph?
items.answer: NetworkX is used for the knowledge graph, specifically as a directed graph (nx.DiGraph) in storage/knowledge_graph.py.

items.question: What is the confidence_threshold for routing in config.yaml?
items.answer: The confidence_threshold for routing is 0.6, configured under the router section of config.yaml.

items.question: How many agents are in the multi-agent system?
items.answer: There are 3 agents: code_agent, data_agent, and reasoning_agent. The router selects among them based on query type.

items.question: What cross-encoder model is used for final reranking?
items.answer: The model cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2 is used for cross-encoder reranking, configured by reranker_model in config.yaml.

items.question: What is the maximum chunk size in tokens as configured?
items.answer: The maximum chunk size is 512 tokens, configured by max_chunk_tokens under the chunking section of config.yaml.

items.question: Which orchestration framework is used to wire the agent pipeline?
items.answer: LangGraph is used to orchestrate the pipeline, defined in agents/orchestrator.py using StateGraph with route, retrieve, and agent nodes.

items.question: How does the router decide to escalate a query to the reasoning agent?
items.answer: The router escalates to reasoning_agent when the normalized confidence score is below the confidence_threshold (0.6). Confidence is computed as the best-matching agent's score divided by the total signal across all agents.

items.question: What is the overlap_tokens setting for sliding window chunking?
items.answer: The overlap is 64 tokens, configured by overlap_tokens under the chunking section of config.yaml.

items.question: What edge types are used in the knowledge graph schema?
items.answer: The knowledge graph uses seven edge types: Calls, Imports, Inherits, Uses, Creates, Reads, Writes. These are defined in storage/knowledge_graph.py.

items.question: What LLM backend is configured in config.yaml?
items.answer: The ollama backend is configured via llm_backend: ollama in config.yaml. The abstraction layer in llm/backend.py routes calls to ollama_backend.py.

items.question: What function in ingestion/loader.py is used to load a repository?
items.answer: The load_repository function in ingestion/loader.py is used to load a repository. It yields file_info dicts with file_path, content, repo_name, and branch fields.

items.question: How many top-K results does the vector search retrieve before reranking?
items.answer: The vector search retrieves 30 results (vector_top_k: 30), which are then reranked to 5 (rerank_top_k: 5), as configured in config.yaml under the retrieval section.

items.question: What node types are defined in the knowledge graph schema?
items.answer: The knowledge graph has four node types: Functions, Classes, Files, and Modules. These are added in storage/knowledge_graph.py based on chunk metadata.

items.question: What chunk metadata fields are stored per chunk?
items.answer: Each chunk stores: repository, branch, language, file, class, function, lines, hash, and timestamp as metadata fields.
items.notes: Verifiable from PROJECT_SPEC.md section 7 and ingestion/chunker.py

items.question: What reasoning model is used by the reasoning agent?
items.answer: The reasoning agent uses qwen2.5:3b-instruct, configured by reasoning_agent_model in config.yaml. The agent calls llm.backend.generate() with this model key.
